title: Trump Laid Out Five Goals for the Iran War. Here’s Where They Stand.
summary: The United States and Israel have done significant damage to Iran’s military capabilities. But Iran still fires missiles, has nuclear material and coordinates with militias in the region.

title: Iran Is Skeptical About Diplomacy, U.S. Intelligence Says
summary: Any decision by Iran to keep fighting would complicate President Trump’s stated goal of trying to end the war within weeks.

title: How NASA Is Keeping the Astronauts Safe During the Artemis II Mission
summary: Years of testing, emergency systems and sensors have gone into protecting the astronauts set to head to the moon.

title: Trump Has Discussed Firing Attorney General Pam Bondi
summary: President Trump has not made a final decision, but he has floated the idea of replacing Ms. Bondi with Lee Zeldin, the E.P.A. administrator.

title: Bondi Is Vulnerable as Republican Frustrations Over DOJ’s Epstein Files Missteps Grow
summary: Ms. Bondi’s critics inside and outside the administration say she has made unforced errors that have turned the Justice Department’s handling of the Epstein files into a political crisis.

title: D.H.S. Inspector General Inquiry Focuses in Part on Corey Lewandowski
summary: The investigation comes as administration officials have fielded complaints about how Kristi Noem’s top adviser, Corey Lewandowski, dealt with companies seeking federal contracts.

title: Key Justices Appear Skeptical of Limiting Birthright Citizenship
summary: President Trump appeared in court, watching as members of the court’s conservative majority raised questions about his efforts to limit birthright citizenship.

summary: The justices grappled with questions about domiciles and foundlings, avoided policy debates and mused about the sweep of possible rulings.

title: Trump Attends Supreme Court Oral Arguments, Then Leaves an Hour In
summary: President Trump’s presence in the court, a presidential first, put him face to face with justices whom he has tried to bully and intimidate.

summary: As the high court heard arguments on whether to limit birthright citizenship, many people pondered what the decision could mean for future generations.

title: Tech Bros Hacked Their Diets. Now You May Be Doing It, Too.
summary: With seeds, supplements and gadgets (but little expert guidance), Americans of all stripes are seeking wellness through what they eat.

title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX Files to Go Public, Setting Stage for Huge I.P.O.
summary: An initial public offering of Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite company would most likely be a generational wealth event and one of the largest offerings ever.

title: Who Murdered Minerliz? How a Diner Meet-up Cracked the Case.
summary: In 1999, a 13-year-old girl was found dead in the Bronx. Then the trail went cold for 20 years, until an ingenious ploy by detectives produced a vital clue to her killer.

title: Ocasio-Cortez Says She Will Oppose All U.S. Military Aid to Israel
summary: Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York said she would oppose U.S. military aid to Israel, including for defensive systems.

summary: Representative Julia Letlow of Louisiana, running to challenge Senator Bill Cassidy, is facing conservative blowback over remarks from 2020.

title: Syracuse Drops 84 Programs Including Classics, Ceramics and Italian
summary: In all, 93 of the 460 academic programs at the university will be closed or paused. No students were majoring in 55 of the programs that are ending.

title: Lawsuits Are the New Trump Tactic in the Fight to Overhaul Education
summary: Trump officials have faced dozens of lawsuits over their aggressive efforts to force change in universities and school districts. Now Trump lawyers are taking schools to court.

title: Goodbye ‘Geeky Hunk’? Gmail Users Can Now Change Their Usernames.
summary: Users who have been saddled with now-cringe email handles since the mid-2000s can now change them without losing any data under a policy Google announced on Wednesday.

title: DNA Confirms Ted Bundy Killed Utah Teen in 1974, Investigators Say
summary: Mr. Bundy had confessed to killing Laura Ann Aime before he was executed in 1989. Investigators said DNA testing provided conclusive proof.

title: What to Know About Ted Bundy, the Notorious Serial Killer
summary: On Wednesday, the authorities said DNA evidence had linked Mr. Bundy, who was executed in 1989, to the murder of a 17-year-old Utah girl in 1974.

title: Medical Examiner Rules That a Rohingya Refugee’s Death Was a Homicide
summary: An autopsy showed that the man suffered dehydration and hypothermia after Border Patrol agents dropped him off on a cold night in Buffalo. Gov. Kathy Hochul condemned their actions as cruel and inhumane.

title: A.I. Companies Shatter Fund-Raising Records, as Boom Accelerates
summary: OpenAI, Anthropic, Waymo and other artificial intelligence companies hauled in $297 billion in funding in the first three months of the year.

title: Can Science Predict When a Study Won’t Hold Up?
summary: Conducting research is hard; confirming the results is, too. And artificial intelligence isn’t yet ready to help, a major new study finds.

summary: The case is open-and-shut on the merits. But its procedural course still leaves plenty of room for Trump administration overreach.

summary: Higher oil prices and limited supply will accelerate a turn toward cleaner technologies, especially in Asia and Europe.

title: Key Justices Appear Skeptical of Limiting Birthright Citizenship

title: Man in Custody After 7-Month-Old Girl Is Fatally Shot in Brooklyn
summary: The baby was struck by a stray bullet on Wednesday afternoon in the Williamsburg neighborhood. The police said they were still looking for another person.

title: Migrating American Woodcocks Set Off a Birding Frenzy in NYC’s Bryant Park
summary: American woodcocks, also known as timberdoodles, are bopping and shimmying through the park, charming visitors during a pit stop on their northerly migration.

title: Mahmoud Khalil Asks Emil Bove to Recuse Himself From Immigration Case
summary: Mr. Khalil, who is fighting his deportation, and Judge Bove were on opposite sides of President Trump’s crackdown on campus protesters when the judge was a Justice Department official.

title: Tennessee Library Director Luanne James Fired Over L.G.B.T.Q. Books
summary: The director, Luanne James, was fired at a board meeting for the Rutherford County Library System on Monday after she refused to move certain books to the adult section.
